questions.question: According to AFH 1, when is change appropriate?
options: When leadership directs it regardless of circumstances
When there is a perceived gap between what the norms are and what they should be
When the majority of employees vote for it
When the organization has been static for more than one year

questions.question: What is the best quality a leader can demonstrate when coping with change?
What is an essential element for preventing or overcoming resistance to change?
options: Offering financial incentives to all employees
Establishing a well thought out plan
Replacing employees who resist the change
Delaying the change until all resistance is eliminated

questions.question: Which common response to change occurs when organizational structure is so rigid that it inhibits change and possibly damages professional relationships?
What is the role of the 'change agent' in organizational change?
options: The person who resists organizational change
The person advocating for or leading the organizational change
The outside consultant who evaluates the change after implementation
The person responsible for documenting the change in official records

questions.question: Which approach to reducing resistance to change can negatively affect attitudes and potentially cause long-term negative consequences?
Who proposed the three-stage change process model of unfreezing, changing, and refreezing?
What is the most neglected, yet essential part of the unfreezing stage?
options: Creating an environment where people feel the need for change
Identifying the change agent for the project

questions.question: During Stage 2 (Changing) of the three-stage change process, what does the changing stage involve modifying?
options: Only personnel assignments and duty titles
Technology, tasks, structure, or distribution of people

questions.question: During the refreezing stage, what is a critical step?
options: Immediately beginning a new change initiative
Returning to the old process if any issues arise
Remaining engaged and evaluating results to determine if the change reached the desired effect

questions.question: What does Continuous Improvement (CI) increase while reducing associated costs?
Which CI methodology is focused on workflow, customer value, and eliminating process waste, with a primary focus on eliminating non-value-added activities?
Which CI methodology is a rigorous, data-driven approach focused on minimizing waste through identifying, controlling, and reducing process variation?
How many steps are in the Practical Problem-Solving Method?
In the Practical Problem-Solving Method, what should a problem statement NOT assume?
options: The scope and direction of the problem
A root cause, solution, or countermeasure
The who, what, when, and where of the problem

questions.question: According to the Practical Problem-Solving Method, what is the most commonly neglected step?
options: Step 1: Clarify and Validate the Problem

questions.question: What is the most common mistake made during Continuous Improvement efforts?
What are the three levels of effort available for the Practical Problem-Solving Method?
options: Just Do It, Rapid Improvement Event, and Improvement Project

questions.question: What are the three main constraints most often realized in project management?
What is a work breakdown structure?
options: A list of employees assigned to each phase of a project
A schedule showing the timeline for project completion
A technique based on dividing a project into sub-units or work packages
A risk assessment document identifying potential project failures
